meta.subject: Civic Education
meta.philosophy: The Civic Education Syllabus has been reviewed by the need to provide a national age-appropriate curriculum for learners from Form 1 to 4. The syllabi aim to provide quality education that is aligned with the Competence-Based Curriculum and twenty-first Century Skills. It also aims to impart the knowledge, skills, values and positive attitudes that are aimed at enabling learners to live and grow into productive and useful members of their communities and the Zambian society.
meta.competence_learning: Learners are at the centre of all teaching and learning activities and this is the primary focus of learner-centred teaching. Learners are encouraged to actively and independently engage in their education. The teacher facilitates learning by building on a learner's past knowledge, skills, values, attitudes and experiences. Recommended methodologies include: Individual work, Teamwork/Group work, Question and answer, Field trips, Exploration, Discussion, Inquiry, Pairs, Project work, Research.
goals: Analyse process of Constitution making and adoption in Zambia
Describe the role of Electoral Commission of Zambia (ECZ)
Respect human dignity and examine categories of Human Rights
Differentiate between local and international trade
Justify significance of selecting leaders in any organisation
Use banking and financial services offered by commercial banks

specific_outcomes: Analyse process of Constitution making and adoption in Zambia
Analyse the role of the Electoral Commission of Zambia (ECZ)

subtopics: The concept of Economic and Social Development
The role of Informal Sector to National Development
specific_outcomes: Devise solutions in dealing with life challenges
Demonstrate understanding of the role of Informal Sector to National Development
Differentiate between local and international trade

subtopics: Role of the United Nations in promoting peace
specific_outcomes: Promote peace and non-violence in society
Examine the role of United Nations in promoting peace

subtopics: Specialised Agencies of the United Nations
specific_outcomes: Demonstrate understanding of Specialised Agencies of the United Nations Organisations
Interpret terms of conditions of borrowing

subtopics: Financial Services offered by Commercial Banks
